provinces.name: Phnom Penh
provinces.description: The capital city known for its culture, history, and riverside views.

provinces.name: Siem Reap
provinces.description: Iconic gateway to the largest temple complex in the world, the Angkor Wat Temple.

provinces.name: Preah Vihear
provinces.description: Home to a world's heritage crowned by a spectacular clifftop temple.

provinces.name: Battambang
provinces.description: Artistic riverside town famous for colonial charm and the bamboo train.

provinces.name: Sihanoukville
provinces.description: Cambodia's premier coastal hub, offering turquoise waters and pristine tropical islands.

provinces.name: Kampot
provinces.description: Peaceful riverside town famous for durian farms, pepper farms and sunsets.

provinces.name: Kep
provinces.description: A quiet seaside escape renowned for its fresh crab and sunsets.

provinces.name: Kratie
provinces.description: Home to the rare, endangered Irrawaddy dolphins in the Mekong River.

provinces.name: Koh Kong
provinces.description: An eco-tourism frontier defined by vast mangrove forests, waterfalls, and the dense Cardamom Mountains.

provinces.name: Mondulkiri
provinces.description: Cool highland sanctuary of rolling hills, thundering waterfalls, and ethical elephant encounters in the wild.

provinces.name: Ratanakiri
provinces.description: Famous for the emerald-green Yeak Laom Volcanic Lake, indigenous hill-tribe cultures, and Virachey National Park.

provinces.name: Banteay Meanchey
provinces.description: A bustling border province that hides the massive, jungle-entwined ruins of the Banteay Chhmar temple complex.

provinces.name: Kampong Cham
provinces.description: A friendly Mekong hub featuring the historic bamboo bridge, ancient temples, and rubber plantations.

provinces.name: Kampong Chhnang
provinces.description: Famous for its traditional handmade pottery and sprawling floating villages of the Tonle Sap lake.

provinces.name: Kampong Speu
provinces.description: A lush outdoor destination favored for the pine forests and cool mountain air of Kirirom National Park.

provinces.name: Kampong Thom
provinces.description: The archaeological heart of the country, home to the pre-Angkorian temples of Sambor Prei Kuk.

provinces.name: Kandal
provinces.description: The historic spirit of the capital's outskirts, the former royal capital of Cambodia.

provinces.name: Oddar Meanchey
provinces.description: A remote northern frontier where the rugged Dangrek Mountains located.

provinces.name: Pailin
provinces.description: A gem-mining hub, known for its beautiful hills, waterfalls, and the glittering Wat Phnom Yat.

provinces.name: Prey Veng
provinces.description: A serene agricultural heartland steeped in early Khmer history and the sacred legends of Ba Phnom.

provinces.name: Pursat
provinces.description: Experience the great lake of Kampong Luong floating town and visiting the remote Cardamom Mountains.

provinces.name: Stung Treng
provinces.description: A rugged northern crossroads where the Mekong's flooded forests provide a sanctuary for kayakers and nature lovers.

provinces.name: Svay Rieng
provinces.description: A bustling border province with a unique local culture and the scenic Waiko River meanders that captivate the heart of visitors.

provinces.name: Takeo
provinces.description: Often called the Cradle of Khmer Civilization, it features the ancient hilltop temple of Phnom Chisor and the Angkor Borei archaeological site.

provinces.name: Tboung Khmum
provinces.description: Cambodia's newest province, known for its vast rubber plantations and the historical Haong Waterfall.
